_meta.status: RESOLVED — O3 accepted with documentation addendum (decision-sheet-v1.md, 2026-05-11)
_meta.o3_resolution: 8 abstraction names confirmed: 5 ResourceBudget sub-types + ToolAvailability + SubGoalTransition + InformationState. CoordinationDependency and OptimizationCriterion excluded per EC-07 (no violation rules apply).
_meta.rule_bearing_vs_dimensional: Six of the eight abstractions are RULE-BEARING — symbolic checker rules exist: ResourceBudget_HP (R3/R4/R8/R9), ResourceBudget_PP (R1), ResourceBudget_time (R1), ToolAvailability (R2/R6/R10), SubGoalTransition (R11), InformationState (assessment-only as of v5.3 but rule-eligible). Two are DIMENSIONAL-ONLY — no symbolic checker rules apply: ResourceBudget_status (decay=none/turn_based; no rule), ResourceBudget_boost (decay=none; no rule). The dimensional-only abstractions appear in the schema for completeness of the abstraction space. They will not generate violation events in the test set, and Lever 13 L2 (top-3 by importance or frequency) will naturally exclude them under either selection criterion. This asymmetry is a documented feature, not a defect.
schema_constraints_satisfied: additionalProperties: false on all object definitions
every property listed in required array
no minLength / maxLength / minItems / maxItems / patternProperties / numerical bounds

function.name: analyze_battle_chain
function.description: Analyze a Pokemon battle chain for constraint violations. Return structured assessment across the 8 abstraction dimensions.

violation_detected.description: True if any rule violation is detected in the chain; false otherwise.

abstraction_assessments.description: Assessment for each of the 8 abstraction dimensions. All 8 must be populated.

ResourceBudget_HP.description: HP resource constraint. Checks for hp_resurrection (R3/R8/R9) and faint_no_permanence (R4). NOTE: HP events have decay='none' in V1 translation layer — R1 (monotone_increase) does NOT apply to HP. Resource names: hp_unit_X. Amount is normalized 0.0-1.0.

violated.description: True if an HP constraint is violated in the chain.

turn_index.description: Turn number where violation occurs. Use -1 if no violation.

ResourceBudget_PP.description: PP (move uses remaining) resource constraint. Checks for monotone_increase (R1). Resource names: pp_action_N_own, pp_action_N_opp. Amount is normalized 0.0-1.0.

ResourceBudget_time.description: Match time remaining resource constraint. Checks for monotone_increase (R1). Resource name: match_time_remaining. Amount is normalized 0.0-1.0.

ResourceBudget_status.description: Status condition resource constraint. Resource names: status_unit_X. Amount is severity (par/brn=0.5, slp/frz=1.0, psn/tox=0.3). Decay is 'none' for most statuses; 'monotone_decrease_if_turn_based' for sleep/toxic. R1 (monotone_increase for monotone_decrease resources) does NOT apply to most status events. V5.3 symbolic checker may not have an explicit rule for status violations.

ResourceBudget_boost.description: Stat boost resource constraint. Resource names: boost_STAT_unit_X (e.g. boost_atk_unit_A). Amount is magnitude of boost/unboost normalized 0.0-1.0 (stage/6). Decay is 'none'. NOTE: R1 (monotone_increase for monotone_decrease resources) does NOT apply since decay='none'. V5.3 symbolic checker may not have an explicit rule for boost violations.

ToolAvailability.description: Unit availability constraint. Checks for dead_unit_returns (R2), double_availability (R6), causal_incoherence R10. Tool names: unit_A through unit_F. States: available / unavailable.

SubGoalTransition.description: Battle phase transition constraint. Checks for causal_incoherence R11 (initial-phase re-entry after battle started). Fields: from_phase, to_phase, trigger.

InformationState.description: Information reveal state constraint. Checks for consistency of observable_added sequences. No direct symbolic checker rule as of v5.3, but included as an assessment dimension.

primary_violation_abstraction.description: Name of the abstraction dimension where the primary violation occurs. Must be one of the 8 abstraction keys, or 'none' if no violation detected.

supporting_evidence.description: Quote or paraphrase of the specific chain event(s) supporting the violation assessment. Empty string if no violation.

_validation_notes.thinking_mode_constraint: Lever 12 L3 (strict function calling) requires thinking mode DISABLED. Empirical finding (O4 diagnostic, 2026-05-12): on the /beta endpoint, model=deepseek-v4-flash defaults to thinking-mode enabled, and `tool_choice='required'` is server-rejected ('deepseek-reasoner does not support this tool_choice') under that default. Explicit `extra_body={'thinking': {'type': 'disabled'}}` resolves it while preserving the SPEC's named target model. This constraint is benign in the OLAT matrix because Lever 18 baseline (L1) already disables thinking; the explicit setting is documentation of an implicit prerequisite, not a new methodology constraint.
_validation_notes.lever_17_override: When testing Lever 12 L3, Lever 17 baseline (free-form YES/NO) is overridden. The boolean YES/NO answer is returned as violation_detected field within this schema.
_validation_notes.lever_18_l4_interaction: When Lever 18 L4 (native thinking) is active, reasoning_content must be preserved and re-injected across sub-turns within a single user question. Dialogue manager must not strip reasoning_content between tool result and follow-up.
remaining_pre_signoff: O4: Test schema against DeepSeek /beta endpoint with a dummy call before finalizing
